title: ConvertKit vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and AWeber. Find pricing, features, and which email marketing platform suits your needs best.
intro: Both ConvertKit and AWeber are established email marketing platforms offering free tiers and creator-friendly features, but they serve different audiences and strengths. ConvertKit targets content creators and course sellers with visual automation and commerce tools, while AWeber prioritizes simplicity for bloggers and solopreneurs. This guide breaks down their key differences to help you choose the right platform for your business.
choose_a_if: You're a content creator, course seller, or digital product business needing commerce and advanced automation
You want visual workflow builders and prefer a modern, creator-focused interface
You're willing to invest more for powerful tagging and subscriber segmentation features
choose_b_if: You're a beginner or solopreneur wanting simplicity and affordability
You need an established platform (founded 1998) with strong reliability and customer support ratings
You want to get started quickly with fewer features and lower monthly costs

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ConvertKit excels for creators and course sellers with advanced automation and commerce features, earning a higher G2 rating (4.4 vs 4.2). However, AWeber scores better on Trustpilot (4.0 vs 3.2) and offers greater simplicity for beginners. ConvertKit is the better overall choice if you need modern features; AWeber wins if you prioritize ease of use and cost.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or AWeber?
faq.a: AWeber is more affordable, starting at $15/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Additionally, AWeber's free tier supports 500 subscribers while ConvertKit allows 1,000. However, both offer free plans, making them accessible entry points. Your actual costs will depend on subscriber growth and required feature complexity.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists, though the process varies. Most users can export contacts as CSV files and import them into ConvertKit. It's recommended to back up your automations and sequences before switching, as migration doesn't transfer workflows automatically. Both platforms offer customer support to help with the transition if needed.
